creator: dinoex@FreeBSD.org
text:
State Changed
From-To: open->feedback


Sorry, I can not reproduce the problem you see. 

I use virtusertable extensivly, and it works as expected. 

Please check that your have "mydomain.com" as local host name defined. 
e.g. in "/etc/mail/local-host-names" 

The local user must work without virtusertable, 
check with this line: 

$ sendmail -d0.1 -bv simonp@mydomain.com 
[...] 
simonp@mydomain.com... deliverable: mailer local, user simonp 

$ pkg_info -W /usr/local/sbin/sendmail 
/usr/local/sbin/sendmail was installed by package sendmail+tls+sasl2-8.14.8 

Please verfify the database format of your "virtusertable.db" 

$ file /etc/mail/virtusertable* 
/etc/mail/virtusertable:        ASCII text 
/etc/mail/virtusertable.db:     Berkeley DB 1.85 (Hash, version 2, native byte-order) 
/etc/mail/virtusertable.sample: ASCII English text 

Please check the output for the version of berkely db, normally none. 

$ ldd /usr/local/sbin/sendmail 
/usr/local/sbin/sendmail: 
libwrap.so.6 => /usr/lib/libwrap.so.6 (0x33d40000) 
libsasl2.so.3 => /usr/local/lib/libsasl2.so.3 (0x33d48000) 
libssl.so.8 => /usr/local/lib/libssl.so.8 (0x33d61000) 
libcrypto.so.8 => /usr/local/lib/libcrypto.so.8 (0x33dbb000) 
libutil.so.8 => /lib/libutil.so.8 (0x33f39000) 
libc.so.7 => /lib/libc.so.7 (0x33f4a000) 
libthr.so.3 => /lib/libthr.so.3 (0x34068000) 

Please check the "/var/log/maillog" for any errors.
